title: YOUTH SHELTERS - 211info
text: CORVALLIS HOUSE YOUTH SHELTERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency and transitional shelter with access to basic needs (bed, food, shower, laundry, hygiene, clothes, etc.). Length of stay depends on individual needs and engagement in the program. Individuals participate in case management, skill-building, education, mental health supports, and family mediation (if appropriate). Access to activities, mentoring, academic coaches, and employment support. 555 NW Jackson Avenue Corvallis, OR 97339 Eligibility: -Ages 10-17 runaway, unaccompanied, or otherwise homeless or at risk -Youth living outside the service area will not be turned away -Must be attending a state-certified education program. If not enrolled in school upon arrival, the agency will support accomplishing this. -Cannot serve youth with a fire-starting history, actively suicidal, or adjudicated sex offenders due to licensing Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 754-2404 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (541) 754-2404 Main phone Main phone (541) 754-2404 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in, call, or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Copy of health insurance, and social security number if applicable. Bring medications in prescription bottles. LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Benton. Lincoln, Polk, and Yamhill Counties Services: Youth Shelters If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. CORVALLIS HOUSE
